title: GATE redesign (Option B): stop repurposing contract haulers; build the gate fleet from income, gated by the shared solvency floor
description:
Admiral decision 2026-07-12 (gate-econ investigation): the repurpose-contract-haulers-to-construction behavior is WRONG — it kills income, treasury sits at the material solvency floor, materials PARK, gate never builds. CORRECT: keep the whole contract fleet earning; build the gate delivery-hauler fleet from income; fleet buys respect the SAME shared EffectiveReserveFloor the material engine uses (so fleet + materials can't starve each other). Chosen: Option B (bootstrap owns the buy + borrows the floor), delivery-haulers-only, shared floor, retire min_contract_earners.

CHANGE PLAN (from gate-econ, cited):
COORDINATOR run_bootstrap_gate.go: (1) planGateWorkers (:52-100) delete ReleaseShips/keep/surplus (:63-71); desired=min(chains+1,GateWorkerTarget); buy=1 when desired>obs.GateWorkers (drop +len(release), :88) — ALL bought. (2) sizeGateWorkers/actGate (:272-285,:127-153) remove repurpose loop (:277-279); retire repurposeHauler (:290-326) + twinreport.Report('repurpose') (:325). (3) maybeBuyGateWorker (:378-395) swap price<=reserve_margin*treasury for treasury - common.EffectiveReserveFloor(cfg.Reserve,cfg.ReservePct,treasury) >= price+margin (shared floor, same as production_executor.go:401,426; reserve_floor.go = max(50k,min(abs,40%*treasury))). (4) retire WorkerRepurposer plumbing (run_bootstrap_coordinator.go:143-149,249,311-313 + adapter bootstrap_ports_gate.go:264-276). (5) retire min_contract_earners (whole fleet earns). (6) derivePhase comment (run_bootstrap_reconcile.go:210-215) update rationale.
GO UNIT TESTS (largest edit): run_bootstrap_gate_test.go — TestBootstrap_PlanGateWorkers_RepurposesSurplusFirst(:63), keep-earner(:91), seed-covers-deficit(:125), fakeRepurposer(:199) wired into ~8 handler tests → all-bought; retire fakeRepurposer.
E2E SPECS (5/9 + 1 new): gate-worker-sizing repurposed->0/bought=D/contract-intact; gate-restart-idempotency+double PurchaseShip 2->D; gate-worker-cap +repurposed===0+contract-intact; gate-golden-path +contract-earning-at-COMPLETE+widen budget; NEW gate-solvency (low credits, fleet buys PAUSE at floor — teeth via twin per-PurchaseShip credit debit ships.ts:213 — contract fleet stays earning). D computed from obs.GateMaterialChains (twin serves 2-line GATE_MANIFEST on /v2, NOT fixture.gateMaterialChains).
SEED daemon-seed.ts seedDaemonGateEntry: haulers STAY contract earners (comment inversion); VERIFY obs.HasIdlePurchaser stays true for the larger all-bought count; add low-credits fixture.
TWIN: none required (source:'bought' + credit debit already served ships.ts:215-216,213). Material-funding solvency has NO teeth (construction-supply never debits credits routes/construction.ts:88-92) — scope the solvency spec to FLEET buys only.
Blast: CONTAINED — ~4 go prod files + the unit-test cluster + 5 specs + light seed. Supersedes the repurpose parts; keeps st-drm.19 observation fixes. Orchestrator runs live GATE after.
notes:

D=3 (obs.GateMaterialChains=2 from the /v2 2-line GATE_MANIFEST + 1 delivery, cap 6). GATE suite running live now to confirm: no repurpose op, all workers bought, PurchaseShip===3, fleet-buy pauses at shared floor max(50k,40%*treasury)+resumes, contract fleet earning+intact through GATE incl COMPLETE, gate reaches done. st-drm.19 observation fixes kept underneath.
2026-07-12 GATE live 1/10 (only gate-sticky). Option B LOGIC WORKS (heartbeat: 'gate worker buy decision: price=300000 treasury=3000000 reserve_floor=…=1000000 affordable=t' — solvency guard correct; contract fleet stays HAULER/contract, no repurpose op). But the BUY EXECUTION fails: 'gate worker purchase failed: … navigate to shipyard: set flight mode: API error 404 Ship TWINAGENT-H1 not found'. ROOT CAUSE: the D1-seeded contract haulers (TWINAGENT-H1..) exist ONLY in the daemon :5434, NOT the twin world.ships (twin world.ships=[TWINAGENT-1,2] only). title: HANDOFF: resume st-drm (twin + bootstrapper) in a fresh context — read before bd ready
description:
Read this first, then `bd show st-drm` and `bd ready`. The prior session built + verified the twin thoroughly but over-fanned subagents and never ran INCOME/GATE. This is the clean resume point. cwd for bd = the `city/` dir (resolves the st- DB).

## GOAL (epic st-drm)
Captain-bootstrapper (sp-ysgb) drives a COLD agent DATA→INCOME→GATE→COMPLETE — builds the jump gate AUTONOMOUSLY (zero human/AI intervention), MINIMUM wall time — PROVEN by bootstrap-harness (25 e2e specs) vs a CORRECT twin of /v2.
TRUST: harness > bootstrapper code > design (all fallible). Red harness ⇒ default-suspect the coordinator (gobot), fix it. Twin must match the REAL API (cross-check official docs, not just the Go-client decode).

## WHERE THINGS LIVE
- TWIN (Node+TS+Fastify, /v2 mock + /_twin admin, :8080, seeded era-2 X1-PZ28): worktree `/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/captain-worktrees/twin-digital-twin/twin`, branch `feat/twin-digital-twin` (23 src files; NOT merged — main's `twin/` is only the fixture-capture harness, 0 src).
- HARNESS = THE ORACLE (25 e2e: DATA 8, INCOME 9, GATE 8 + 3 unit): `spacetraders/bootstrap-harness` on main. Boots its OWN isolated daemon → twin via ST_API_BASE_URL; drives `workflow bootstrap`; asserts /_twin/state + daemon Prometheus.
- GOBOT (daemon+CLI): worktree `.../twin-digital-twin/gobot`. bin/ built. ST_API_BASE_URL seam in client.go. twinreport.go = /_twin/report emission (test-gated, prod no-op).
- TEST INFRA: Postgres :5434 (spacetraders_test); daemon socket /tmp/spacetraders-daemon-test.sock. HARD-RESET 5434 (TRUNCATE all tables CASCADE) between manual live-stack runs — state leaks across back-to-back runs. PROD daemon (DO NOT TOUCH): /tmp/spacetraders-daemon.sock → real orion.spacetraders.io.
- Run a harness phase: start twin (`cd twin && ./node_modules/.bin/tsx src/main.ts`), seed TWINAGENT (`spacetraders player register --new --agent TWINAGENT --faction COSMIC --socket <test-sock>` with ST_API_BASE_URL→twin, DATABASE_URL→5434), then `cd bootstrap-harness && ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/<phase>/` with HARNESS_GOBOT_DIR→worktree gobot, HARNESS_TEST_DATABASE_URL→5434. (Prior session's `run-harness-phase.sh` is in this job's scratch tmp — may not survive; recreate from these steps.)

## DONE (committed on feat/twin-digital-twin; st-drm.3 + .5 CLOSED)
- ALL twin /v2 bootstrapper endpoints: register/agent/ships[list/show/purchase/navigate/orbit/dock/refuel/PATCH-nav]/markets/shipyards/waypoints/contracts[negotiate/get/accept/deliver/fulfill]/cargo[purchase/sell]/construction[get/supply].
- Responses shape-conformant to real 2.3.0 openapi.json via non-mutating `twin/src/world/serialize.ts` + has-teeth OpenAPI sweep `tests/openapi/shape.test.ts` (24 tests) — EXCEPT construction (→ st-drm.9).
- navigate consumes fuel (fidelity, clock.ts `fuelCostFor`). 207 in-process unit green (`vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts`).
- Behavior acceptance (`twin/tests/acceptance/`): contracts-lifecycle (in-proc) ✓, construction (in-proc) 9/9 ✓, cargo-trade (LIVE) ✓. ship-actions (LIVE) 3/5 — refuel + purchase RED (→ st-drm.12).
- Report seam (6 ops) ✓. **DATA harness 8/8 GREEN vs twin.**

## THE WAVES (bd deps encode this — `bd ready` = current wave)
- WAVE 1 (READY NOW, parallel): **st-drm.8** time-mock · **st-drm.9** OpenAPI construction cases · **st-drm.10** harness theatre-audit · **st-drm.11** CLI-acceptance audit + write-missing.
- WAVE 2: **st-drm.12** crafter → ALL low-level acceptance green (blocked by .8 + .11).
- WAVE 3: **st-drm.6** run harness INCOME→GATE→COMPLETE to green (blocked by .8 + .10 + .12). ← THE GOAL.
- WAVE 4: **st-drm.7** min-wall-time optimization (blocked by .6).

## st-drm.8 TIME-MOCK — DO FIRST, CRITICAL DESIGN
- Frozen-time is WRONG: the daemon's ship_state_scheduler arms `time.AfterFunc(time.Until(arrival))` on REAL wall time and AUTONOMOUSLY detects arrivals; the bootstrapper depends on that. Frozen arrivals ⇒ daemon must become twin-aware = theatre.
- USE compression: `arrival = wall_now + real_eta/compression`. Make compression configurable in the twin (env, 1×/10×/100×); travel inversely proportional to compression.
- TWO 1s floors block sub-second nav: (a) the twin's own `max(1000ms, eta/compression)` in clock.ts; (b) the daemon's ~1s ClockDriftBuffer clamp. BOTH must drop via ONE SHARED seam (prod=1000ms, test≈50ms, ADJUSTABLE). The daemon clamp is a gobot change (AUTHORIZED, like ST_API_BASE_URL). RISK: the seam value must stay ≥ the daemon's poll granularity AND the twin's compressed arrival must land ≥ the clamp, or the daemon MISSES arrivals — test carefully. Objective: daemon runs as if real API; nav fast at ANY distance.

## PARALLELIZATION RULES (hard-won this session — obey them)
- Test-WRITING (nw-acceptance-designer) PARALLELIZES: disjoint test files, NO commits (orchestrator collects+commits at the end), NO src edits, NO live-stack runs. In-process inject tests are in-memory (bind no port) ⇒ safe concurrent.
- LIVE-STACK (:8080) is a SINGLETON ⇒ ONE run at a time, done by the ORCHESTRATOR (main loop). Subagents hit the 180s no-tool-call STALL on slow live-stack/harness runs — never have a subagent run them.
- Implementation (nw-software-crafter, src edits): serialize on shared files (server.ts/types.ts/clock.ts) OR isolated worktrees + merge. Verify IN-PROCESS (fast, no stall).
- ANTI-STALL (all subagents): Bash timeout ≤120s/command; NEVER `find /`; NEVER spawn a manual twin/daemon (globalSetup boots it); one live-stack file at a time. vitest.unit.config.ts = in-memory; default vitest.config.ts = live-stack (globalSetup).

## NO-CLI ENDPOINTS (cannot have low-level CLI acceptance — don't invent commands)
Contract lifecycle (negotiate/accept/deliver/fulfill) + construction supply = automation-only (batch-contract + construction-executor containers). Flight-mode (PATCH nav) = no CLI. Their oracle = in-process (inject) + the harness. st-drm.11 "write missing CLI" applies ONLY to CLI-testable endpoints.

## LOOSE ENDS
- ship-actions 2 RED: refuel (dock-at-remote-waypoint timing — LIKELY resolves once st-drm.8 time-mock lands) + purchase (player-info credit source — daemon read-back). → st-drm.12.
- OpenAPI construction shape cases missing → st-drm.9.
- Twin branch feat/twin-digital-twin: pushed at handoff (see git). One uncommitted test edit (ship-actions purchase credit-check, F1) may be committed as an honest RED or reverted by st-drm.12.
notes:
WAVE-1 WORKFLOW (authored 2026-07-12, NOT launched — launching it is the next session's FIRST move):
- Script: /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/captain-worktrees/twin-digital-twin/.claude/workflows/st-drm-wave1.js (committed on feat/twin-digital-twin; full launch + merge protocol in its header comment).
- Launch: from a session whose cwd is INSIDE that worktree, call Workflow({scriptPath:'<the path above>'}). Pre-check: git merge-base --is-ancestor main HEAD (branch was rebased onto main 2026-07-12 and force-pushed; main was pushed too — origin main=dca1095f). Do NOT launch while any live twin/daemon run is active (:8080, /tmp/spacetraders-daemon-test.sock, :5434 are global singletons).
- It runs st-drm.8 (time-mock, opus) + .9 (openapi construction, sonnet) + .10 (harness theatre audit, opus/nw-acceptance-designer) + .11 (CLI acceptance audit+write, opus/nw-acceptance-designer) in FOUR ISOLATED WORKTREES; every agent commits in its own worktree and reports branch+SHAs+diffstat.
- AFTER the wave, the orchestrator (not agents): verify each diffstat non-empty (uncommitted worktree merges EMPTY), merge sequentially A(time-mock)->B(openapi)->D(cli)->C(harness) into feat/twin-digital-twin with --no-ff + diffstat check after each, run twin unit suite + go build, wire bootstrap-harness/tests/helpers/daemon.ts with the env knobs track A reports, push, update beads .8-.11.
- THEN Wave 2 (st-drm.12) and Wave 3 (st-drm.6 harness INCOME->GATE->COMPLETE) — live-stack/harness runs stay in the orchestrator main loop.
WAVE ARTIFACTS (all committed on feat/twin-digital-twin under .claude/workflows/):
- st-drm-wave1.js — WORKFLOW, 4-track parallel fan-out in isolated worktrees (.8/.9/.10/.11).
- st-drm-wave2.js — WORKFLOW, triage -> adaptive fan-out: one agent partitions the merged low-level suite into DISJOINT file-groups, then parallel nw-software-crafters (isolated worktrees) fix each; orchestrator merges + confirms the live-stack reds (.12).
- st-drm-wave3-runbook.md — RUNBOOK (NOT a workflow): harness INCOME->GATE->COMPLETE (.6). Live stack is a singleton; the orchestrator runs it in its main loop.

DELEGATION DISCIPLINE (applies to the NON-WORKFLOW / orchestrator-loop portions — Wave-2 post-merge live-stack confirmation AND all of Wave 3): the orchestrator MUST DELEGATE every twin/gobot/coordinator code fix to a SUBAGENT (nw-software-crafter in its own isolated worktree, verified in-process / targeted go test, then orchestrator merges with diffstat check + rebuilds). The orchestrator writes NO production code inline — it owns ONLY the singleton live-stack/harness RUNS, the MERGES, and the RE-RUNS. This keeps 'orchestrate, don't implement' intact even where the phase is not a parallel fan-out; it also keeps each subagent's context clean and lets independent hypotheses/fixes run in parallel worktrees when a red allows it.

title: Validate sp-64se receipt-ranking on live re-solved caps: sp-94du re-solve IS live but new J58 set has none of the high-value contract-reward goods (still ELECTRONICS, dropped MEDICINE)
description:
VALIDATION HANDOFF (captain -> economy-analyst; you own depot whitelist / receipt-demand config + before/after measurement). sp-94du + sp-64se deployed; daemon rebuilt 16:40 local (19:41 UTC).

## sp-94du: CONFIRMED LIVE (the re-solve works)
Running warehouses recomputed caps on restart — storage_operations.updated_at now 19:41 UTC (fresh, not the stale 17:01), and J58 != central (no longer the identical generic set). The IsIdle-skip fix landed.

## sp-64se: the re-solved set looks WRONG — needs your judgement vs the CURRENT contract slate
Live supported_goods (19:41 UTC):
- J58 (TORWIND-11, 12): [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, FABRICS, POLYNUCLEOTIDES]
- central (TORWIND-A): [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, POLYNUCLEOTIDES]

This set does NOT contain the high-value contract-reward goods sp-64se was meant to promote — CLOTHING (~149k), ASSAULT_RIFLES (~102k), FIREARMS (~101k) are all ABSENT. It still carries ELECTRONICS (zero J58 contract history per sp-64se — a red flag it is not contract-reward-derived) and DROPPED MEDICINE (historical #1 by reward). POLYNUCLEOTIDES (the single lowest-value whitelist good) is retained.

## ASK
1. Validate the ranking against the CURRENT contracts (contracts.deliveries_json / ContractGoodDemand), NOT sp-64se's historical snapshot — are [ELECTRONICS,EQUIPMENT,FABRICS,POLYNUCLEOTIDES] genuinely the current top J58 contract-reward goods, or is depotReceiptPayment still falling back to market-ask because the reward rows are empty (the cold-start reward-gap sp-94du flagged)? ELECTRONICS' presence strongly suggests the fallback.
2. Measure served-from-buffer now that sp-kqxe withdrawal instrumentation is LIVE (sp-94du notes 1 row so far).
3. If it is the reward-data-gap, that is a follow-on shipwright bead (populate/thread the contract-reward rows) — you own the diagnosis + the before/after measurement.

## DATA POINTERS
storage_operations.supported_goods (above, player-3); contracts.deliveries_json (current, player-3); warehouse_withdrawals (sp-kqxe). Related: sp-64se, sp-94du (both shipwright), sp-537w (aggregate-split, stays blocked until the ranking is confirmed correct).
notes:
ECONOMY-ANALYST CONSULT ANSWER — st-0dh (validate sp-64se re-solved caps vs current slate + measure served-from-buffer). Live data 2026-07-14, player-3, X1-VB74.

## RECOMMENDATION
Your instinct is right: the re-solve works but the set is wrong — and I filed the fix as sp-fo0d (P1, shipwright).

1. RE-SOLVE CONFIRMED (sp-94du): caps recomputed on reload (updated_at 19:41, J58 != central). That fix is good.
2. RANKING IS NOT REWARD-DRIVEN — dispositive proof: the J58 set keeps POLYNUCLEOTIDES (reward/unit 373, the LOWEST of all 37 contract goods) and drops MEDICINE (6935), CLOTHING (6642), DRUGS (7039), ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY (8352). Selection is INVERSELY correlated with reward-per-unit. No correct contract-reward ranking does that.
3. PARTIAL VINDICATION of your "check vs current slate": the slate HAS shifted. FABRICS is now genuinely the top recent contract good (3 in last 6h, 189k avg — highest of any good), and EQUIPMENT is current (1 active). So FABRICS + EQUIPMENT are DEFENSIBLE picks. But ELECTRONICS (0 recent, zero J58 history) and POLYNUCLEOTIDES (lowest value) are NOT justified by any current-slate reading.
4. ROOT CAUSE = upstream of depotReceiptPayment. I checked the code: depotReceiptPayment (HEAD 9f8be3b4) correctly prefers ContractRewardPerUnit with market-ask fallback — so the reward field IS consulted. The bug is that the high-reward goods aren't reaching the knapsack with a populated reward. Narrowing: even the market-ask fallback ranks MEDICINE (~3608) above POLYNUCLEOTIDES (~197), so POLYNUCLEOTIDES-in / MEDICINE-out means MEDICINE is almost certainly ABSENT FROM THE MINED CANDIDATES — a demand-miner scope/window issue, not merely an empty-reward fallback. (Details + hypotheses + code pointers on sp-fo0d.)
5. SERVED-FROM-BUFFER (sp-kqxe live): only 2 withdrawals so far, both EQUIPMENT (32u, 19:00-19:56) — far too thin to measure cycle-time compression yet. It confirms only stocked goods get served, so this coverage bug directly costs the high-value contracts (MEDICINE/CLOTHING/FABRICS). I will run the before/after the moment the ranking is fixed and withdrawals accrue.

NET: don't trust the current J58 buffer as representative of sp-64se's intent — it's coverage-wrong (missing the high-value goods) for the reason on sp-fo0d. sp-537w (aggregate-split) stays blocked until the ranking is fixed, as you noted.

## EVIDENCE
- Live supported_goods (storage_operations, 19:41): J58 [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, FABRICS, POLYNUCLEOTIDES]; central [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, POLYNUCLEOTIDES].
- Contract demand by good (reward/unit, count, recent): FABRICS 3322/17/3-recent-189k-avg; EQUIPMENT 4368/20/1-active; ELECTRONICS 3648/12/0-recent; POLYNUCLEOTIDES 373/20/0-recent | vs excluded MEDICINE 6935/11, CLOTHING 6642/10, DRUGS 7039/4, ADV_CIRC 8352/4, ASSAULT_RIFLES 5417/5, FIREARMS 5575/5.
- Code: depotReceiptPayment prefers ContractRewardPerUnit (wired); RewardPerUnit derived in history_repository.go (rewardSum/units); miner in demand_miner.go.
- warehouse_withdrawals: 2 rows, EQUIPMENT 32u.

## CONFIDENCE
HIGH the ranking is not reward-driven (inversion is dispositive; code shows the selector is wired -> upstream). MEDIUM-HIGH it is a miner scope/window issue over a pure empty-reward fallback.

## WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND
If the receipt miner is intentionally windowed to a very recent slate AND that window genuinely contains ELECTRONICS/POLYNUCLEOTIDES contracts but not MEDICINE/CLOTHING (I could not confirm a window that includes POLYNUCLEOTIDES but excludes MEDICINE — both are 0 in 6h) — then the set would be current-correct and the fix is instead to widen/reward-weight the window. Shipwright's code trace on sp-fo0d settles it.
RESOLVED by economy-analyst. Ranking confirmed NOT reward-driven (J58 keeps POLYNUCLEOTIDES @373/u, drops MEDICINE/CLOTHING/DRUGS @6935-8352/u). Re-solve (sp-94du) works. FABRICS+EQUIPMENT defensible (slate shifted, FABRICS now top @189k); ELECTRONICS+POLYNUCLEOTIDES not. Root cause UPSTREAM: receipt miner scope/window drops high-reward goods; depotReceiptPayment wired right. Analyst filed sp-fo0d (P1 shipwright). Served-from-buffer too thin to measure yet (2 EQUIPMENT withdrawals). Closing consult; fix tracked in sp-fo0d.
CORRECTION (economy-analyst, after full pipeline trace): my diagnosis above (reward-gap / miner scope-window) was incompletely right. The reward WIRING is correct; the real root cause is that the depot receipt path reuses the source-side DemandMiner.Mine, which sorts candidates by buy-leg SAVINGS + truncates to topN=20 BEFORE the receipt-reward knapsack (demand_miner.go:240-256) — so contract-reward only reorders the savings-selected survivors. sp-fo0d's era filter is a NO-OP for this data (prior eras have zero X1-VB74 contracts). Filed sp-wxf2 (P1, shipwright) with the code location + fix (rank the depot candidate set by contract-reward, not savings). Forcing a re-solve does NOT help (superseded my captain note st-wisp-cg6tf).
RESOLVED (economy-analyst 2026-07-14): sp-wxf2 (69bfd480) deployed the traced fix (RankBy: depot ranks receipt candidates by contract-reward, stocker keeps savings). RE-VERIFIED live: J58 supported_goods now = [ANTIMATTER,CLOTHING,ELECTRONICS,EQUIPMENT,FIREARMS,MACHINERY,MEDICINE,SHIP_PARTS,SHIP_PLATING]; central top-5 by reward. POLYNUCLEOTIDES dropped, high-reward goods (SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS/ANTIMATTER/MEDICINE/CLOTHING) in — matches the traced reward ranking. Fix chain complete (sp-cftm wiring -> sp-64se reward -> sp-94du re-solve -> sp-wxf2 candidate ranking). title: Parallel test-harness: per-worker stack isolation (twin port + daemon socket/ports + Postgres DB) for vitest fileParallelism — general twin infra
description:
Admiral 2026-07-12: the twin will back OTHER feature suites beyond the bootstrapper, so per-worker parallel isolation is worth the ~1-day build. Full per-worker (general), NOT the 3-phase-stack shortcut. Goal: vitest fileParallelism with each worker owning an isolated stack -> ~W x wall-clock; TRANSPARENT to specs (no spec/scenario/seed changes).

THREE SINGLETONS TO ISOLATE, keyed on VITEST_WORKER_ID (1..N):
1. Twin (Fastify :8080) -> per-worker port (e.g. 8080 + 10*W).
2. Daemon -> per-worker unix socket (/tmp/st-daemon-harness-{W}.sock), gRPC (50072+W), metrics (9092+W), pidfile.
3. Postgres (:5434 spacetraders_test) -> per-worker DB spacetraders_test_{W}.

MECHANISM:
- config.ts: compute per-worker DEFAULTS for API_BASE_URL/ADMIN_URL/TEST_DAEMON_SOCKET/gRPC/metrics/TEST_DATABASE_URL from VITEST_WORKER_ID; KEEP all HARNESS_* env overrides (manual single-stack runs still work).
- globalSetup (once, before workers): ensure spacetraders_test_template exists + migrated (apply the daemon's migrations once); for W in 1..maxForks: DROP+CREATE DATABASE spacetraders_test_{W} TEMPLATE spacetraders_test_template (fast fully-migrated clone).
- per-worker setup (setupFiles, module-singleton per worker keyed on port): launch this worker's twin (tsx src/main.ts on its port + knobs) if not up; health-wait; register TWINAGENT against it (writes players/eras+token into spacetraders_test_{W}); store proc, kill on worker exit + globalTeardown sweep.
- per-spec (UNCHANGED): startTestDaemon boots the daemon on THIS worker's socket+DB; resetDaemonDb truncates THIS worker's DB (keep players/eras); /_twin/reset seeds THIS worker's twin; seedDaemonMarketCoverage/GateEntry target THIS worker's DB.
- vitest.config.ts: fileParallelism:true; pool:'forks'; poolOptions.forks.maxForks=min(cpus-2,6); globalSetup+globalTeardown; setupFiles per-worker twin/register.
- teardown: drop spacetraders_test_{1..N}; sweep leftover twins/daemons/sockets.

TRANSPARENCY: specs+helpers already read HARNESS_API_BASE_URL/HARNESS_ADMIN_URL/HARNESS_TEST_DATABASE_URL/TEST_DAEMON_SOCKET -> parallelization only changes their DEFAULT computation + adds lifecycle. No spec changes; GATE + future suites inherit it free. Applies to BOTH bootstrap-harness/ and twin/tests/ (share the config/setup).

ACCEPTANCE: serial baseline green -> parallel run same specs green, ~W x faster; no cross-worker bleed. Timing-race specs (mid-transit) may flake more under load -> retry:2 covers.

FILES (merge-orthogonal to spec-level GATE work): config.ts (per-worker derivation), new global-setup-parallel.ts + per-worker-setup.ts + globalTeardown, vitest.config.ts, daemon.ts (socket/ports/DB via config.ts), a DB-template script. NO product code.

SEQUENCING: implement on a FULLY-GREEN baseline (after GATE) so acceptance = 'serial N green -> parallel N green'; avoids churning the harness under active GATE bring-up + a 3-way merge.

title: GATE two coordinator/twin code bugs: (a) pipelineStatusAdopted misclassifies PLANNING as adopted; (b) COMPLETE never derives
description:
gate-preflight finding 2026-07-12. Two REAL code bugs (not seed work) blocking GATE specs, beyond the daemon-local observation seeds (st-drm.6).

BUG A [BLOCKER, gate-construction-start + gate-golden-path]: pipelineStatusAdopted (gobot/internal/adapters/grpc/bootstrap_ports_gate.go:139-150) uses a status vocabulary (PENDING/CREATED/NEW=not-adopted vs ACTIVE/RUNNING=adopted) that does NOT match the real pipeline enum (PLANNING/EXECUTING/COMPLETED/FAILED/CANCELLED — manufacturing/pipeline.go:15-27,116). A fresh pipeline is PLANNING -> falls to default -> adopted=true -> the L57 executor-bounce is skipped -> the twin construction.adopted (set only by the executor-bounce report) never flips. FIX: pipelineStatusAdopted must treat PLANNING as NOT-adopted.

BUG B [BLOCKER, gate-golden-path + gate-monitor-complete + gate-handoff + gate-restart-*]: COMPLETE never derives. (a) twin setConstruction(100) writes world.construction.percent + world.constructionIsComplete (admin.ts:248-249) — fields NO /v2 endpoint serves; the daemon reads completion only via /v2 GET construction (materials-based), so the specs' completion trigger is a no-op to the daemon. (b) readBootstrapGateSnapshot does 'if site.IsComplete() { continue }' (bootstrap_ports_gate.go:110) BEFORE setting snap.Complete/Site -> a completed gate makes the snapshot go empty and the phase REGRESSES out of GATE instead of ->COMPLETE. FIX: serve completion via a /v2 path that setConstruction drives (twin), AND stop continue-ing past a complete gate before recording snap.Complete/Site (gobot). Twin construction GET+supply themselves are truthful (serialize.ts:209 isComplete=materials.every(fulfilled>=required)); a real supply chain CAN drive materials->isComplete — the daemon just can't observe completion. Both need TDD fixes; dispatch after INCOME seed lands. Related: st-drm.6, st-drm.18.
notes:
2026-07-12 gate-code DONE (branch worktree-agent-a5e2b7d74454c9a9c, commits 2479af72+f0b669e9, 4 files +227/-33; go build+go test green [grpc 53 files, bootstrap], twin unit 226 green; NOT merged). BUG A: pipelineStatusAdopted explicit allowlist — EXECUTING+terminal=adopted, PLANNING/empty/unknown=NOT-adopted (fail-safe: skip-bounce stalls, spurious-bounce self-corrects) so ensureExecutorAdopted (run_bootstrap_gate.go:207) runs the L57 bounce -> construction.adopted flips. BUG B(b1,twin admin.ts): setConstruction(100) marks every served constructionMaterial fulfilled=required (same state a real supply chain reaches) so GET /v2 construction isComplete=true; frozen /_twin/state view untouched. BUG B(b2,gobot): extracted selectGateTarget — prefer first under-construction gate but surface a solely-complete scan as complete=true (not dropped by the old 'if IsComplete continue') so derivePhase->COMPLETE. Tests: TestPipelineStatusAdopted (all enum values), TestSelectGateTarget (prefer-incomplete + complete-not-dropped), construction.test.ts extended. MERGE PENDING: gate-seed still on the shared stack/twin/binary — merge gate-code (gobot+twin) + rebuild shared bin + RESTART twin (admin.ts change) + re-register TWINAGENT ONLY after gate-seed completes. Then live GATE run confirms: construction.adopted flips + gate reaches COMPLETE/done.
2026-07-12 MERGED into feat/twin-digital-twin (merge after f31e97ca); go build green, daemon rebuilt, twin restarted to pick up admin.ts. GATE suite running live now.

title: Steppable world-clock reshape: frozen+advanceMs, faithful travel/cooldown durations, world-time-to-COMPLETE metric
description: Replace the 100x compression clock with a controllable clock (POST /_twin/clock) driving arrivals + mutation-log 'at'. Travel/cooldown durations must match the real API so min-wall-time optimization transfers. Expose elapsed world-time-to-COMPLETE in /_twin/state. Prereq for navigate/cooldown acceptance.
notes:
WALL-TIME (design SILENT on time-to-gate = headroom; preserve capital-gate+idempotency). Biggest lever: coordinator.go:347-351 always sleeps cfg.Tick even after action ticks (~10 buys≈10 ticks≈50min sleep at 300s) -> act immediately when last tick did work. Then: buy-N-per-tick/batch yard trip (BatchPurchaseShipsCommand supports Quantity>1, hardcoded 1 at bootstrap_ports.go:432); overlap phases/soften bars; shrink 1h realized-income INCOME floor; hub selector never ranks by travel distance (contract_hub_selector.go:40). Twin must simulate FAITHFUL travel/cooldown durations so gains transfer to real API. GATE snapshot dep: twin construction endpoints must populate PipelineID/PipelineStatus/Materials (adopted iff status ACTIVE/IN_PROGRESS/RUNNING/COMPLETED) or GATE stalls.
CALIBRATE travel/fuel/cooldown from prod tour_leg_telemetry (4201 real legs, localhost:5432 READ-ONLY) — empirically confirm the travel-time-vs-distance/flightmode/speed formula rather than guessing from docs.
REAL-API TRAVEL FORMULA (fidelity agent, full: scratch api-fidelity-spec.md) — CORRECTS Wave-1 clock: travelSeconds=round(round(distance)*(mult/engineSpeed))+15; mult CRUISE 25/BURN 12.5/DRIFT 250/STEALTH 30; distance=round(sqrt(dx^2+dy^2)). Wave-1 used WRONG constants (31/26/15/50) AND omits +15. Calibrate CRUISE 25-vs-30 via one live navigate route.departureTime/arrival OR prod tour_leg_telemetry. fuel/mode: CRUISE round(d)/BURN 2*round(d)/DRIFT 1 flat/STEALTH round(d). CRITICAL: probes fuel.capacity=0 consume NO fuel — navigate ALWAYS allowed; a required=distance model 4203-blocks probes and breaks DATA golden-path.
CLOCK-DETERMINISM TENSION (key remaining challenge for reliable DATA). The daemon detects ship arrival on REAL wall-clock (ship_state_scheduler time.Until(arrival)+1s buffer). The harness advances /_twin/clock FASTER than real (1000ms world / ~300ms real). So: (a) WORLD-clock arrivals (original design) => daemon's real wait = arrival(world)-real_now, which GROWS unboundedly as the harness advances ahead of real => late buys never complete. (b) REAL-clock arrivals (current, compressed) => daemon wait short+consistent, BUT status flips on real time => non-deterministic vs the harness's world-clock poll cadence => FLAKY on timing-sensitive scenarios (staging distinct-world-time-per-buy, restart mid-purchase, coverage-exit, golden-path 2-buys-in-window). Tuning compression (tried 1000 => 1s hops) did NOT fix it. ALSO: daemon emits 'ship version save conflict' (sp-60ff CAS retry) between buys, adding latency/nondeterminism. OPTIONS to resolve: (1) harness advances world 1:1 with real (mode running / smaller advanceMs) so world≈real and both models align; (2) make daemon arrival detection poll-based/world-clock-aware (reduce arrival_wait resync grace, gobot change); (3) fixed-real-hop + widen harness pollUntil budgets. Needs a deliberate choice — do NOT rush.
PATH TO RELIABLE 8/8 (deterministic clock): the two-timestamp scheme. navigate returns route.arrival = real_now + ~1s (short, so the daemon's scheduler timer fires fast + re-polls); the twin tracks a SEPARATE world-arrival = getNow()+compressedTravel; GET /my/ships status flips IN_TRANSIT->IN_ORBIT on getNow()>=world-arrival (DETERMINISTIC, harness-controlled via advanceClock), and while in-transit keeps returning a rolling real_now+1s so the daemon re-arms + re-reads every ~1s until the harness advances the world clock past world-arrival. This makes arrivals harness-deterministic while keeping the daemon's real-timer short. Alternative: gobot daemon poll-based arrival (reduce arrival_wait resync grace, config-gated). Current real-clock model = 7/8 typical, ~10% per-scenario flake from timing variance + daemon save-conflict retries (sp-60ff).

title: EPIC: Bootstrapper flawless autonomous min-wall-time gate build, verified by bootstrap-harness against a correct SpaceTraders twin
description:
## Objective

The captain-bootstrapper (sp-ysgb) drives a cold SpaceTraders agent DATA→INCOME→GATE→COMPLETE — building the system jump gate **autonomously (zero human/AI intervention)** and in **minimum wall time**. Proven end-to-end by the already-built `bootstrap-harness/` (25 e2e specs) running against a **correct digital twin** of the SpaceTraders API.

Handoff origin: st-wisp-m9otd (harbormaster inbox).

## Trust hierarchy (iterate-to-green)

Trust the **bootstrap-harness > the bootstrapper code** (both may be wrong). Consult the **bootstrapper design** as tie-breaker (also fallible). A red harness scenario ⇒ default-suspect the coordinator (gobot) and fix it. The twin must faithfully match the **real SpaceTraders API** (cross-check against the official API docs, not just the Go client's decode targets).

## Architecture

- **Twin** (`twin/`, branch `feat/twin-digital-twin`, worktree `captain-worktrees/twin-digital-twin`): Node+TS+Fastify in-memory mock of `/v2`, seeded from the real era-2 home system **X1-PZ28**, on `:8080`. Serves `/v2` (game) + `/_twin` (admin/test control). Deterministic **steppable world-clock** (frozen + `advanceMs`) with **faithful travel/cooldown durations** so min-wall-time optimization transfers to the real API.
- **Harness** (`bootstrap-harness/`, on `main`, BUILT): self-contained, boots its own **isolated daemon** pointed at the twin via `ST_API_BASE_URL`, drives `workflow bootstrap`, asserts vs `/_twin/state` + daemon Prometheus metrics. The oracle.
- **Verification layering:** FIRST prove each twin endpoint via **low-level CLI-command acceptance** (the twin's own tests) — the contract oracle. THEN run the harness on the proven twin (one unknown per red, not three).

## Key decisions (Admiral: "make all decisions")

- **`/_twin/report` ingest:** 6 daemon-internal ops (fleet-unassign, batch-contract, construction-start, executor-bounce, launch-autosizer/siting/worker-rebalancer) are NOT `/v2`-observable. The coordinator POSTs them to `/_twin/report` (**test-gated** — only when its API base is the twin; prod unchanged); twin appends the mutation-log entry + flips the paired state flag **exactly-once** (restart-idempotent). Design-aligned (heartbeat/decision events).
- **Clock:** frozen + `advanceMs` **replaces** the 100× compression model; `now` drives ship arrivals AND every mutation-log `at` stamp (the "distinct world-time per buy" staging invariant).
- **Ports:** harness/daemon DB retargeted `:5433`→`:5434` (5433 taken by another project); daemon socket/pid `-harness`, gRPC `:50072`, metrics `:9092`.
- **Endpoint waves run sequentially** in the integration worktree (they share `server.ts`); per-agent worktrees reserved for genuinely-disjoint work.
- **Model routing:** Sonnet 5 for mechanical tasks; Opus / Fable 5 for complex (clock, `/_twin` plane, coordinator).
- **`repurpose`** is state-only (`gateWorkers[].source:'repurposed'`, no `PurchaseShip`, no log entry).

Full `/_twin` build reference + exact state/clock/reset shapes: captured in the session scratch `twin-admin-contract.md`.

## Pipeline / status

- Wave 0 (scaffold, `ST_API_BASE_URL` seam, X1-PZ28 capture, harness scripts, fixtures) — **DONE, green**.
- Wave 1 (loader, mintToken, registerAgent, clock[compression], errors, store, buildServer, `/_twin` admin[baseline], global-setup) — **DONE, green** (60 unit tests).
- Wave 2 (DATA `/v2` endpoints: register/agent/ships/waypoints/market/shipyard) — **IN PROGRESS**.
- Wave 5 (INCOME/GATE `/v2` endpoint plan) — **authoring**.
- Infra: test Postgres `:5434` up; CLI + daemon built from the worktree gobot.

See child issues for the remaining work streams.
notes:
FIDELITY DATA SOURCE (Admiral): prod DB localhost:5432/spacetraders (READ-ONLY) has rich real-play data to mine when docs/numbers are in doubt: tour_leg_telemetry(4201, real travel-time-vs-distance/flightmode/speed + fuel -> calibrates the clock st-drm.2 + wall-time formula), market_price_history(51k)+market_data(3996)+transactions(24823, real prices + do-they-move-on-trade + spreads), contracts(854, real terms/payment/deadline/deliver), manufacturing_pipelines(353)+manufacturing_tasks(622)+gate_edges(271, real construction material qty + pipeline states), ships(121, real engine speed/fuel/cargo/frame). Mine these to calibrate twin numbers; prefer observed data over formula guesses.
REAL-API fidelity spec captured (scratch api-fidelity-spec.md, from OpenAPI v2.3.0 + docs + cross-check). Harness-green (levers stub economy) vs real-fidelity (dynamic prices, presence-gating) separated: prioritize green, then fidelity refinements. Travel formula corrected on st-drm.2; error enum on st-drm.1/.3.

OLD: L56 [d-88] — To measure manufacturing/trading standalone $/h: `ledger list --type SELL_CARGO` isolates it CLEANLY — contracts emit CONTRACT_FULFILLED and NEVER SELL_CARGO, so gross trading revenue = sum(SELL_CARGO) with zero hand-pairing (only the BUY side stays muddied, L45). TWO gotchas: (a) the ledger displays in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC — align a sell to its task by the exact 3h offset (sell 21:53:20 local == task-complete 00:53:20 UTC); (b) a factory's FIRST collection dumps ACCUMULATED inventory, so the first COLLECT_SELL task's rate OVERSTATES steady state (task eebee24b: +229,740 rev / ~68,856 buy = ~+160k net in 4min — do not annualize one draw, L41).

title: Consult: J58 buffer diversity — stocking only 2 of 6 whitelist goods (EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE)
description:
CONCERN (economy-analyst — you own the depot whitelist / receipt-demand stocking config): the live J58 depot buffer is stocking only 2 of its 6 whitelist goods. Raising for a read on whether that is correct or a mis-tune.

## OBSERVED (live warehouse contents, player-3, 2026-07-14)
J58 group (TORWIND-11 + 12, 90/160 aggregate):
- EQUIPMENT 50, MEDICINE 40.
- ZERO of the other 4 whitelist goods: ASSAULT_RIFLES, FIREARMS, CLOTHING, POLYNUCLEOTIDES.
Central (TORWIND-A, 45/80): EQUIPMENT 25, MEDICINE 20 — same 2 goods; its stocker reports "nothing to stock — at target / unaffordable / unreachable / stale."

## THE CONCERN
The depot's whole value is BROAD coverage of the receipt-demand goods, so ANY J58-destination contract can withdraw-local (haul-leg off the critical path). If the buffer stays EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE-only, contracts for the other 4 whitelist goods get NO cycle-time benefit — they still source fresh/far (the 696u haul). A narrow buffer = the win lands for only 2 of 6 good-classes.

## QUESTION
Is this:
(a) EXPECTED early-stage — stockers working the cheapest/nearest goods first, will diversify as they cycle; or
(b) the receipt-demand selector CORRECTLY gating the other 4 out (genuinely thin-recurrence / unaffordable / unreachable → they should NOT be buffered; the contract worker sources them fresh); or
(c) a MIS-TUNE — selector under-diversifying, should force coverage across the whitelist?
If (c): how to tune (per-good caps, coverage-vs-depth weighting, a min-per-good floor)?

Note: sp-kqxe (withdrawal instrumentation) hasn't landed, so we can't measure served-from-buffer directly yet — but this coverage gap is directly visible in the warehouse contents. Related: depot config consult st-8rn.
notes:
ECONOMY-ANALYST CONSULT ANSWER — st-rfa (J58 buffer stocking only 2 of 6 whitelist goods). Live data 2026-07-14, player-3, X1-VB74.

## RECOMMENDATION — it's (c) a MIS-TUNE, with two concrete root causes. Not (a) benign early-stage, not (b) correct-gating.

### Why not (b) correct-gating
The 4 missing goods are NOT thin/unaffordable/unreachable. Live receipt-demand + source markets:
- FIREARMS: 5 J58 contracts, avg reward 100.6k, source E44 fresh 7m, RESTRICTED.
- ASSAULT_RIFLES: 5 contracts, 102.1k, source E44 fresh 7m.
- CLOTHING: 3 contracts, 148.8k (HIGHEST reward of the six), source K83 fresh 5m.
- POLYNUCLEOTIDES: 7 contracts but only 7.9k reward / 197 market ask — genuinely low-value, legitimately last (the ONLY one correct to deprioritize).
All source markets are fresh (5-15m, inside the 75m discipline), affordable, in-system reachable. So nothing gates the 3 high-value goods out.

### Why not merely (a) early-stage
The buffer is DEEP-filled on the top two — EQUIPMENT 50u, MEDICINE 40u — far past one-contract width (~17-19u). It is not spreading and pausing; it is deepening the top-2, so it will NOT self-diversify as it cycles.

### The two root causes (both code-confirmed on deployed HEAD)
1. WRONG VALUE SIGNAL. depotReceiptPayment() ranks goods by MARKET ASK (HomeAsk), not contract reward — the code comment admits it is a stopgap ("A follow-on can thread the true contract reward here"). Market-ask ranks EQUIPMENT #2 (ask 4347, the highest); by CONTRACT REWARD EQUIPMENT is ~#5 and CLOTHING/ASSAULT_RIFLES/FIREARMS (100-149k) rank above it. The market-ask top-2 = MEDICINE+EQUIPMENT = EXACTLY the observed buffer. So the buffer optimizes the good's resale value, not the contract payment its cycle-time compression actually unlocks.
2. PER-HULL, NOT AGGREGATE, CAPACITY + deep-fill. depotWarehouseTargetUnits is solved per warehouse hull over ship.CargoCapacity() (80), not the aggregate 160 the design intended for a broad buffer; and the ongoing fill deepens the top goods past one-contract width instead of enforcing coverage>depth. So breadth is structurally capped and the 2nd hull risks duplicating the same top-2 rather than covering new goods.

### Impact
The buffer compresses EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE contracts only — and by CONTRACT VALUE those are NOT the best two (CLOTHING 149k, ASSAULT_RIFLES 102k, FIREARMS 101k all outrank EQUIPMENT 75k). So the feature currently delivers the haul-leg win for the lower-value slice and misses the higher-value one. This directly caps the J58 cycle-time gain (baseline just taken: J58 median 12.3min vs 3.6min central floor).

### Fix (SHIPWRIGHT code — no config knob does this; targetPerGood is a single number, no per-good coverage floor is exposed)
1. Thread the true CONTRACT REWARD into depotReceiptPayment (replace the market-ask proxy) so the buffer prioritizes the goods whose contracts pay most.
2. Enforce COVERAGE-FIRST: cap each good at one-contract width (MaxContractUnits) and spread across the whitelist before deepening any; solve the receipt knapsack over the AGGREGATE co-located group capacity (160), not per-hull (80).
3. Optional: a min-per-good floor guaranteeing >=1 contract width of each whitelist good.

### Interim (captain, now)
Not catastrophic — EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE are high-recurrence, so a real fraction of J58 contracts still compress; the baseline is set to measure it. Options: (i) accept partial coverage and let me measure the partial compression, or (ii) I file the coverage fix to shipwright now. Recommend (ii) in parallel with measurement.

## EVIDENCE
- Receipt-demand (contracts x deliveries_json dest=J58): MEDICINE 7ctr/115.5k, POLYNUCLEOTIDES 7/7.9k, EQUIPMENT 5/74.7k, FIREARMS 5/100.6k, ASSAULT_RIFLES 5/102.1k, CLOTHING 3/148.8k (excluded hogs AMMONIA_ICE/FOOD/EXPLOSIVES confirmed low-value/big-unit).
- Source freshness (market find --side buy): MEDICINE F49 15m, EQUIPMENT K83 5m, FIREARMS E44 7m, ASSAULT_RIFLES E44 7m, CLOTHING K83 5m, POLYNUCLEOTIDES E45 6m — all fresh/reachable.
- Code (deployed HEAD 07d24665): depotReceiptPayment = HomeAsk (market ask), not contract reward; depotWarehouseTargetUnits solved over ship.CargoCapacity() (per-hull 80); ongoing depot stocker StartStocker(targetPerGood=0) -> source-side resolveWarehouseCaps governs per-pass fill.
- Ranking check: by market-ask x recurrence, top-2 = MEDICINE, EQUIPMENT (matches buffer); by contract-reward x recurrence, order = MEDICINE, ASSAULT_RIFLES, FIREARMS, CLOTHING, EQUIPMENT (EQUIPMENT drops to ~#5).

## CONFIDENCE
- HIGH it is a mis-tune, not correct-gating/early-stage (refuted with live recurrence + reward + source-freshness).
- HIGH on root cause #1 (market-ask vs contract-reward): code-confirmed and the ranking math reproduces the observed buffer exactly.
- MEDIUM-HIGH on #2 (per-hull capacity + deep-fill): per-hull capacity is code-confirmed; the deep-fill-past-cap is inferred from observed 50u vs ~22 cap + the source-side ongoing path.

## WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND
- If the demand miner genuinely cannot surface contract reward, #1 is blocked and market-ask is the best available signal — but coverage-first + aggregate capacity (#2) would still broaden the buffer.
- If warehouse-12 (currently ~empty) fills with the OTHER goods within the hour, that shifts weight toward (a) — but the per-hull-same-knapsack logic makes duplication of the top-2 the likelier outcome; worth a re-check.
ANSWERED (economy-analyst): (c) MIS-TUNE, not early-stage/gating. Root causes code-confirmed HEAD 07d24665: depotReceiptPayment ranks by market-ask not contract-reward (EQUIPMENT #2 by ask, ~#5 by reward; CLOTHING/RIFLES/FIREARMS outrank it); per-hull-80 not aggregate-160 capacity + deep-fill past coverage. No config knob. Filed shipwright bug sp-64se. Analyst measures before/after once fixed.

title: Consult: cluster hull-assignment + contract delivery-pool config (coordinator grabbing un-clustered hulls)
description:
QUESTION (economy-analyst — you own cluster config/topology/sizing per sp-u9xa): with the CENTRAL + J58 contract depots live, how should the fleet's remaining hulls be assigned to clusters, and how should the contract coordinator's delivery fleet be configured so it uses ONLY cluster-assigned delivery hulls, NOT a global idle-hauler grab?

## LIVE STATE (player-3, era torwind-2026-07-12, 2026-07-14; treasury ~6.09M)

Depots LIVE via `contract depot start`:
- central: warehouse TORWIND-A@A1, stocker TORWIND-D@A1, delivery TORWIND-6+9@A1, source-hubs E45/K83/D41/E44/I56.
- j58: warehouses TORWIND-11+12@J58, stockers TORWIND-13+14@J58, delivery TORWIND-15@J58 (warehouse hulls still ferrying into position; stocking begins on arrival). Central warehouse already filling (A at 45/80).

Contract coordinator STARTED (contract_fleet_coordinator-player-3-40bd83a3):
- Depot routing works: active contract cmrkok8d5 (J58 destination) routed correctly to depot delivery hull TORWIND-15 (withdraw-local + deliver-local).
- PROBLEM: its fallback/idle-gap pool grabs every UN-clustered idle light hauler.

Arb (trade coordinator): TORWIND-5, 7.

UN-CLUSTERED loose hulls the coordinator is currently pulling in: TORWIND-E (leftover contract tag), TORWIND-8, 10, B, F. Plus spare TORWIND-C (captain-reserved) and buyer TORWIND-1 (frigate, reserved — not a delivery hull).

## DECISIONS NEEDED
1. Where do the loose hulls (E, 8, 10, B, F) belong? Options: add as additional delivery hulls to central and/or j58; hold for the phase-2 H54/H51 far-clusters; or park idle out of contracts entirely. Give a per-hull → cluster assignment.
2. How should the contract coordinator's delivery fleet be configured so it uses ONLY cluster-assigned delivery hulls (e.g., pin `contract start --dedicated-ships` to 6/9/15) with no general-pool grab — or is there a cluster-exclusive mode we should run instead?

DEADLINE: ASAP — the coordinator is live and mis-grabbing hulls; this gates completing the migration cleanly. Reversible meanwhile (can stop/reconfigure the coordinator on your answer).

refutation: n/a — config/topology consult (economy-analyst is the owner), not a spend decision.
notes:
ECONOMY-ANALYST CONSULT ANSWER — st-8rn (cluster hull-assignment + contract delivery-pool config). Live pulls 2026-07-14, player-3, X1-VB74, treasury 6.44M.

## RECOMMENDATION

### Q2 (the live fire — coordinator mis-grabbing): pin it, don't invent a mode
STOP the current contract coordinator (contract_fleet_coordinator-player-3-40bd83a3) and restart pinned to the depot delivery hulls ONLY:

  spacetraders contract start --player-id 3 \
    --dedicated-ships TORWIND-6,TORWIND-15 \
    --standby-stations X1-VB74-A1,X1-VB74-J58

- `--dedicated-ships` reserves 6,15 EXCLUSIVELY for the contract coordinator (claim-filtered out of every other pool). This IS the "cluster-exclusive mode" you asked about — there is no separate flag. It stops the general-idle grab.
- `--standby-stations A1,J58` makes each idle depot hull HOME to its depot between contracts (nearest-station homing: 6->A1, 15->J58) instead of the coordinator's default post-delivery "balance to a market." That standby homing is what ENFORCES co-location — 15 sits at J58 for withdraw-local+deliver-local; 6 at A1. WITHOUT it the coordinator drifts idle hulls to markets and breaks co-location.
- Two dedicated hulls is enough: contracts are serialized (one active at a time), so one delivery hull is always free — no general-pool fallback is ever exercised.

### Q1 (loose hulls E/8/10/B/F + the redundant 9): NOT delivery hulls
Do not add any loose hull as a delivery hull. Serialized contracts => one depot hot at a time => exactly ONE co-located delivery hull per depot, and SelectDeliveryHull always dispatches deliveryHulls[0], so a 2nd delivery hull per depot is never used. That makes central's TORWIND-9 redundant (permanently shadowed by 6) — free it (drop from the central depot's delivery config; inert even if left).

Surplus to redeploy (6 hulls): 8, 9, 10, B, F, and E (after it finishes its in-flight contract).
- +3 to ARB (trade fleet): arb is under-crewed — 13 lanes clear the 1000/u bid-floor vs only 2 working hulls (5,7). Adding 3 -> 5 hulls captures the near-fill top lanes.
- Reserve 3 for the H54 depot build (warehouse+stocker+delivery = 3 hulls), built when J58 proves out — do NOT build H54/H51 now (gated on J58 result, unmeasurable until sp-kqxe). Park them homed/idle until then.

Mechanics (you execute; I advise): 8/10/B are laden 80/80 and F 43/80 (cargo from the grab) — they must offload (deliver or sell) before reassignment. Let E complete its current delivery before freeing it. TORWIND-1 (frigate/buyer) and C (spare) stay reserved.

### Unowned-destination caveat (worth knowing, not blocking)
Contracts delivering to H54/H51 (no depot yet) fall to the coordinator's default distance pick over the dedicated fleet, which borrows the nearest depot hull — usually 15 (closest to H51/H54) — pulling it off J58 until it returns. Serialized + standby homing self-corrects (15 homes back to J58 after), but if far-destination contracts prove frequent enough to dent J58 cycle-time, pin one dedicated far-floater homed to a mid/far standby to absorb them. Measurable once sp-kqxe lands.

## EVIDENCE
- Fleet (live ship list): central {warehouse A@A1, stocker D@A1, delivery 6@A1}; J58 {warehouses 11+12, stockers 13+14, delivery 15}. Arb: 5,7 (trade, busy). Reserved: 1 (frigate), C. Contract leftover: E (in transit @H52). Loose/unassigned: 8,9,10,B,F.
- Mechanism (deployed HEAD 07d24665): routeContractViaDepot routes by depot OWNERSHIP of the destination; SelectDeliveryHull returns deliveryHulls[0] (no load-balancing, no co-location bias, destination not consulted). `contract start --help`: --dedicated-ships = exclusive reservation; --standby-stations = idle dedicated hull homes to nearest waypoint instead of balancing to a market.
- Arb headroom (market spreads X1-VB74, cache ~5.3h): 13 floor-clearing lanes (MICROPROCESSORS, CLOTHING, LAB_INSTRUMENTS, DRUGS, MEDICINE, JEWELRY, MACHINERY, ASSAULT_RIFLES, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY, FIREARMS, SHIP_PLATING, SHIP_PARTS, ANTIMATTER) vs 2 working hulls.
- Treasury 6.44M — this is a positioning/config decision, not a spend (no 25%-rule gate).

## CONFIDENCE
- HIGH on the coordinator config (pin 6,15 + standby A1,J58): grounded in the deployed CLI help + routing/selector code. Directly and reversibly fixes the grab and enforces co-location.
- HIGH on "no extra delivery hulls / 9 redundant": follows from serialized contracts + SelectDeliveryHull[0].
- MEDIUM-HIGH on the arb redeploy (+3): under-crewing is structural (13 lanes vs 2 hulls), but the spread cache is 5.3h old and per-lane VOL CAP=20 limits throughput; 5 hulls is the prior near-fill figure, not re-measured live.
- MEDIUM on the unowned-destination borrow risk: real mechanism, frequency/impact unmeasurable until sp-kqxe.

## WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND
- A live market re-scan showing the top arb lanes already thin/regen-starved (VOL CAP 20) => arb absorbs fewer than 3; put the surplus fully in reserve.
- Contract-destination history showing H54/H51 (unowned) contracts are frequent => pin a dedicated far-floater now instead of deferring.
- A decision to build H54 immediately (not gate on J58) => the reserve-3 becomes a build-now allocation.
EXECUTED (captain, 2026-07-14): (1) coordinator restarted pinned --dedicated-ships TORWIND-6,TORWIND-15 --standby-stations A1,J58 (stops the grab, enforces co-location); (2) TORWIND-9 removed from central depot delivery role (redundant per SelectDeliveryHull[0]); (3) surplus split: E/B/F -> arb (E added; B/F selling MICRO/ELEC at D42 then joining, background), 8/9/10 -> reserved-for-captain, held for the H54 build; (4) cheap grabbed cargo (EXPLOSIVES/SILICON on 8/10) jettisoned; (5) TORWIND-1 buyer + C spare stay reserved. Buffer-diversity concern raised separately on st-rfa.

A worker's daemon did not create its unix socket within the 20s waitForSocket window (daemon.ts, a serial-single-daemon default) under 4x simultaneous fork/twin/daemon startup. retry:2 does NOT absorb it: isolate:false reuses the same fork's dead worker across all 3 retries, so all 3 fail together. The spec itself (data/disabled.e2e.test.ts) passes reliably SERIALLY (goal suite is 29/29 serial). NOT a bootstrapper bug — a parallel-mode robustness gap. Fix direction: (1) make waitForSocket timeout env-configurable + longer under HARNESS_PARALLEL (e.g. 40-60s) since N daemons contend on CPU at startup; and/or (2) stagger worker daemon starts; and/or (3) reap a slow/failed worker daemon on fork failure so it doesn't leak. Serial default is unaffected, so this is a hardening follow-up, not a blocker.

description: Admiral-directed 2026-07-13: start a FACTORY with 1-2 hulls to make the economy cycle (produce -> sell into import sinks -> deepen depth + resale profit). WHICH GOOD? Assess: (a) economy-cycling value (deep/valuable import sink worth deepening); (b) gate-relevance (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY — gate DELIVERY blocked pending sp-382j, but producing for RESALE is fine); (c) supply-feasibility (map-fixed factory: in-system EXPORT site + in-system inputs, supply-first); (d) input-competition + ROI within remaining era-hours (~6d). Recommend GOOD + SITE (waypoint) + whether 1 or 2 hulls + resale target. LIVE SNAPSHOT ~03:55 (torwind-2026-07-12, player-3, home X1-VB74): treasury ~424k; fleet 5/6/7/9 contracts (hubs E45/K83/D42/F49), 8 trade (ADV_CIRC D42->A4 ~40k/cycle), A=warehouse@A1 + B=stocker (BLOCKED on miner_rows=0), 1 reserved frigate, 2/3/4 scout probes. Available hulls for the factory: TORWIND-B (idle, stocker stalled) +/- a 2nd. Deadline ~45m. Reply bead note (Recommendation/Evidence/Confidence/What-would-change-my-mind) + nudge.
notes:
CONSULT ANSWER — economy-analyst — st-usq (which good for a home factory / economy-cycling)

RECOMMENDATION: Do NOT start a factory at X1-VB74 now. ONE exception: queue a FAB_MATS factory (site F48) to launch the MOMENT sp-382j (gate delivery) unblocks — for the GATE, not profit. Filed a blocked-by-sp-382j follow-up bead so it auto-surfaces then.

EVIDENCE:
- The economy-cycling / "stir sinks to fuel arbitrage" rationale is NOT supported in-data (era-2/player-2 analysis, 57k price snapshots + 4,949 tour legs): trade_volume is a FIXED market attribute (94.7% of market series never change it — stirring has no lever to enlarge a sink); sink regeneration is TIME-based, not trade-driven (untraded intervals mean-revert r=-0.38; trading perturbs, time heals); and markets factories actually touched became WORSE arb targets (~$93k/leg vs ~$181k/leg same-good controls) and recovered SLOWER (factories pin prices by constant dumping). So factories do not fuel arbitrage.
- Absent the stir externality a home factory is NEGATIVE-SUM vs arb: it competes for hulls (arb proven ~90-135k/hr/hull realized) AND dumps into the SAME sinks the arb fleet sells into, dragging arb $/hr.
- Every feasible candidate fails on its own merits: SHIP_PLATING → PARKED (tv-6 sink trips the chain-margin absorption cap, the sp-2dv4 guard working as designed); FAB_MATS → no resale sink (only the gate, currently blocked); ADV_CIRCUITRY / FABRICS / MACHINERY → cannibalize lanes the 8-hull trade fleet already runs (ADV_CIRC→A4 already decayed to ~40k/cycle).
- The factory optimizer's PROFIT-GATE is therefore CORRECT — keep it as-is. (I earlier questioned it on the stir rationale; the data reverses that. Withdrawing the "relax chainMarginSafetyFraction" suggestion.)

BYPRODUCT (arb-routing, not factory): arb $/leg is NON-MONOTONIC in sink size — peaks at mid-tier (tv≈90-120, ~$200-320k/leg) and craters at the most-liquid sinks (tv>150, ~$110k; thin spreads). Route arb to fat-but-not-frictionless sinks; don't chase the biggest.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH that a home factory now is negative-sum vs arb and that stirring didn't help as-run (verified the linchpin myself: trade_volume 94.7% constant; $/leg mid-tier peak). MEDIUM caveat: the IDEAL experiment never ran — factories and arb were in DISJOINT time phases, and no single market was ever stirred hard/consistently enough to trip SpaceTraders' trade_volume-tier mechanic. So this is "stirring didn't help as the bot ran it," NOT "stirring proven impossible."

WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND:
- sp-382j unblocks → gate-material factory (FAB_MATS@F48, ADV_CIRC@D42) becomes the correct move: feeding the gate IS the Phase-1 win, a demand sink arb cannot serve. (Queued via the follow-up bead.)
- A high-margin output with a DEEP sink NOT already worked by the arb fleet appears (none obvious now).
- A deliberately aggressive single-market stir demonstrates a trade_volume tier-up (would reopen the stir thesis).
ANSWERED by economy-analyst + acted: NO home factory now (stir rationale disproven in-data). FAB_MATS gate factory queued as sp-dc5o, blocked-by sp-382j, auto-fires when gate delivery unblocks. Gate FAB_MATS supply meanwhile = depth-3 buy @X1-VB74-F48. Closed by captain.

title: Consult: warehouse design — whitelist, per-good caps/sizing, waypoint for the contract-goods buffer
description:
Refute-first consult (Admiral-directed 2026-07-13). Standing up a WAREHOUSE + STOCKER (2 hulls incoming, TORWIND-10/11: one = warehouse buffer, one = stocker loop). Need the DESIGN (your job):
1. WHITELIST — which contract goods should the warehouse buffer? Doctrine: thin portfolio, coverage>depth (contract draws random across goods); include the fat/high-value tier (draws at multiples).
2. PER-GOOD CAPS / SIZING — target units per good (stocker --target-per-good, or measured-demand default)?
3. WAREHOUSE WAYPOINT — best central in-system waypoint to park the buffer (contract workers withdraw in-system; minimize withdrawal distance)?
4. STOCKER config — budget-per-leg, freshness, priorities?

DEMAND SIGNAL (spacetraders history contracts): current era torwind-2026-07-12 (46 contracts) BY GOOD: POLYNUCLEOTIDES 6, EQUIPMENT 4, MEDICINE 4, ANTIMATTER 3, FABRICS 3, SHIP_PARTS 3, COPPER 2, MICROPROCESSORS 2, LIQUID_NITROGEN 2, + singles (ALUMINUM, CLOTHING, DRUGS, ELECTRONICS, MACHINERY, PLASTICS, QUARTZ_SAND, ...). Prior era torwind-2026-07-05 (824 contracts) top: IRON 56, EQUIPMENT 50, ELECTRONICS 41, LIQUID_HYDROGEN 38, IRON_ORE 38, LIQUID_NITROGEN 37, POLYNUCLEOTIDES 36, SHIP_PARTS 34, COPPER 34, SHIP_PLATING 36, PLASTICS 31, CLOTHING 28.

LIVE SNAPSHOT (~03:35, torwind-2026-07-12, player-3, home X1-VB74): treasury ~938k; fleet 5/6/7/9 contract haulers (hubs E45/K83/D42/F49), 8 dedicated trade (ADV_CIRCUITRY D42->A4 ~40k/cycle), 1=reserved frigate, 2/3/4 scout probes, 10/11 incoming = warehouse+stocker. Contracts serialized (1 active at a time), ~164k/hr gross, fulfill 0.98-0.99.
DEADLINE ~45min. Reply as bead note (Recommendation / Evidence / Confidence / What-would-change-my-mind) + nudge. I set up the warehouse+stocker on your whitelist.
notes:
CONSULT ANSWER — economy-analyst — st-abx (warehouse+stocker design)

Data: player-3, current era torwind-2026-07-12 (46 contracts, 26 goods), fresh DB ~03:40Z. Prior era (824 ctr) used only to corroborate recurrence (EQUIPMENT/SHIP_PARTS/POLY overlap); current-era geometry governs (era resets move sources). SELECT-only.

=== RECOMMENDATION ===
**COUNTS: 2 warehouses (80 cargo each = 160) + 1 stocker to start (scale to 2 stockers on measured far-run starvation).**
WAYPOINT: **A1** (5,27). WHITELIST: 9 goods (below). Rationale: the whitelist's high-value tier is ~155 units at 1-contract depth → doesn't fit one 80-cargo hull; 2 warehouses hold it. Consumption is serialized (1 contract/~8min), so ONE stocker's supply rate exceeds it on central loops — a 2nd stocker is only needed if the far J58/I56 refill legs starve central stock (measurable trigger, not upfront).

WHY A1: contract DESTINATION distribution — A1 is the modal central dest (8 contracts) AND receives the entire fat tier (ANTIMATTER, CLOTHING, EQUIPMENT, JEWELRY, MEDICINE all deliver to A1). Warehouse at A1 = ~0 withdraw+deliver for the money goods. (J58 also gets 8 contracts but those are far *deliveries* no warehouse can compress.)

WHITELIST + CAPS (cap = max observed contract units; serialized ⇒ 1 contract's worth is enough, don't double-depth):
  WAREHOUSE 1 — recurrent core + farthest-source fat (78u):
    MEDICINE   cap 20  (4x draws, src F49)
    EQUIPMENT  cap 20  (4x, K83)
    ANTIMATTER cap  8  (3x, I56 FAR/EXCHANGE — big buy-leg saved)
    SHIP_PARTS cap  6  (3x, D41)
    DRUGS      cap 24  (1x, J58 farthest source — the single largest buy-leg elimination)
  WAREHOUSE 2 — orphan fat goods (no current hub covers them) (81u):
    FIREARMS   cap 20  (1x, E44)
    JEWELRY    cap 24  (1x, H54)  [trim to 23 to hit 80]
    MICROPROC  cap 16  (2x, A3)
    CLOTHING   cap 21  (1x, K83)
EXCLUDE (deliberately): FABRICS/POLYNUCLEOTIDES (hub-covered @E45), FOOD (K83 trade hub), and ALL bulk raws (COPPER/PRECIOUS_STONES/QUARTZ_SAND/ALUMINUM/SILICON/LIQUID_NITROGEN/HYDROCARBON) — low pay per unit + cargo-hogs; buffering them wastes cargo and capital.

STOCKER config (1 hull):
  - Sources (verified in-system, cheapest buy): MEDICINE@F49(1393), EQUIPMENT@K83(1094), ANTIMATTER@I56(EXCHANGE 4921,tv18), SHIP_PARTS@D41(1720,tv6), DRUGS@J58(1524), FIREARMS@E44(1188), JEWELRY@H54(1186), MICROPROC@A3(1237), CLOTHING@K83(1427).
  - --target-per-good = caps above. Reorder when stock < cap (after a contract consumes it).
  - Priority = value-per-cargo-unit: ANTIMATTER(9.0k/u), SHIP_PARTS(8.9k), DRUGS(6.4k), MEDICINE(5.7k) restock FIRST; and PRIORITIZE THE FAR SOURCES (J58 DRUGS, I56 ANTIMATTER) — those are exactly what a contract worker can't self-source quickly, so the warehouse must never be dry on them.
  - budget-per-leg ~45k (covers the priciest cap-refill: ANTIMATTER 8x4921=39k, DRUGS 24x1524=37k).
  - freshness: continuous top-up; central goods (F49/K83/D41/E44/A3/H54, all <=106u from A1) refill fast on one loop.

=== EVIDENCE ===
- 80 cargo ≈ 4–5 goods at 1-contract depth ⇒ full fat/recurrent whitelist (9 goods, ~159u) needs 2 warehouses. WH-1 alone (recurrent core + DRUGS) captures the highest-EV goods; WH-2 adds the orphan fat singles.
- Stocker throughput: consumption is serialized at ~1 good/8min (~30u avg ⇒ ~3.75 u/min). One stocker carrying up to 80u on ~15-min central loops supplies ~5.3 u/min > consumption. So 1 stocker keeps up — EXCEPT during a far run: J58 (DRUGS) ≈ 764u ≈ ~40-min round trip, I56 (ANTIMATTER) ≈ 219u ≈ ~20min. While the lone stocker is east, ~5 central contracts can fire and deplete WH-1. That is the ONLY reason to add stocker #2 → split east-loop (J58/I56/K83/A3) vs central-west-loop (F49/D41/E44/E45/H54). DRUGS draws rarely (1x) so the far run is infrequent — hence 1 stocker to start, measure, scale.
- Whitelist is EV-ranked: recurrent core (MEDICINE/EQUIPMENT 4x, ANTIMATTER/SHIP_PARTS 3x) = highest P(draw)×value; DRUGS/orphan-fat = high value + no hub covers their source.

=== STRATEGIC NOTE (refute-first) ===
The warehouse's real payoff isn't marginal buy-leg savings — it's that it moves the buy-leg OFF the serialized contract critical path (stocker refills in background; contract worker just withdraws+delivers). That lets you CONSOLIDATE the 4 hub haulers (5/6/7/9 @E45/K83/D42/F49): a central warehouse + delivery makes per-good hub-positioning redundant. If the hub haulers redeploy to TRADE (135k/hr each per st-tlg), the warehouse pays for itself many times over — 2 WH + 1 stocker (3 hulls) frees up to 4 hub haulers = net +1 hull to the trade fleet, plus higher-$/hr. If instead the warehouse is ADDED on top of the hubs, its 3-4 hulls carry the opportunity cost of that many trade tours — weaker. RECOMMEND: build the warehouse to REPLACE hub-positioning, not augment it.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
HIGH on waypoint (A1 — dest data is unambiguous) and the recurrent-core whitelist. MEDIUM on the exact counts: 2 WH assumes 80-cargo hulls (confirmed) and 1-contract depth; 1-vs-2 stockers hinges on far-run starvation I can't measure until it runs. MEDIUM on the orphan-fat WH-2 goods (1x draws — a thin sample; they may not recur).

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND ===
- Hubs CONSOLIDATE (haulers→trade): widen whitelist to include the hub goods (FABRICS/MEDICINE/EQUIPMENT/POLY) ⇒ 3 warehouses, and go 2 stockers from the start.
- If the daemon warehouse is VIRTUAL (waypoint storage, not a cargo-capped hull): then it's 1 logical warehouse, size the whitelist freely (drop the 2-hull split) — confirm with shipwright whether buffer capacity = hull cargo or unbounded.
- Measured central stockouts during J58/I56 runs ⇒ add stocker #2 (east/central split).
- Draw distribution shifts (e.g. a fat single starts recurring, or POLY's 6x frequency makes cheap POLY buffering worth a slot).
- If prior-era heavies (IRON/ELECTRONICS/LIQUID_HYDROGEN) reappear this era ⇒ revisit; currently absent, so excluded.
DONE (partial, 2026-07-13): stood up WAREHOUSE-1 on TORWIND-A @X1-VB74-A1 (goods MEDICINE,EQUIPMENT,ANTIMATTER,SHIP_PARTS,DRUGS) + STOCKER on TORWIND-B (--warehouse-waypoint A1 --budget-per-leg 45000 --iterations -1). Both RUNNING (warehouse-TORWIND-A-4b6ae980, stocker-TORWIND-B-c5b5c265). Started with WH-1 (highest-EV core) + 1 stocker = the 2 hulls bought; WH-2 (orphan-fat singles FIREARMS/JEWELRY/MICROPROC/CLOTHING, MEDIUM-conf 1x draws) DEFERRED pending a 3rd hull + treasury recovery. ANALYST STRATEGIC NOTE surfaced to Admiral: build warehouse to REPLACE hub-positioning (redeploy 4 hub haulers 5/6/7/9 to trade @~135k/hr) once warehousing coverage is broad enough — currently WH-1 only covers 5 goods so hubs still needed.

title: income-restart-mid-transit e2e flake: characterize + fix (recovery race vs cross-spec contamination vs kill-timing)
description: income-restart-mid-transit.e2e.test.ts (mid-flight hull re-adoption across a daemon reboot) FAILED 1x in the full 11-spec income serial suite but PASSES in isolation (1/1) -> either an inherent mid-transit recovery race/timing fragility OR cross-spec contamination from a leaking sibling (sticky compression clock left !=200, leaked daemon, or twin/:5434 pollution). Core user requirement: bootstrapper must be impervious to daemon restarts + tests non-theatrical. Delegated live diagnosis+fix (systematic-debugging agent, owns stack): characterize isolation-vs-suite, capture the failing assertion + daemon recovery logs (re-navigate? orphan? re-buy? slow?), fix faithfully (daemon arrival-timer re-arm if real race / arrange-hardening if kill mis-timing / sibling fix if contamination), verify 10x isolation + 3x suite green. NO assertion weakening. Blocks final full-suite green + parallel-harness merge (st-drm.22).
notes: 2026-07-13 DIAGNOSED + FIXED (verifying). ROOT CAUSE (two compounding): (1) REAL twin fidelity bug — twin/src/clock.ts resolveNav served nav.waypointSymbol=ORIGIN while IN_TRANSIT; real-API contract is DESTINATION (daemon maps it to Ship.currentLocation; invariant 'IN_TRANSIT -> currentLocation is destination'). A rebooted daemon re-adopting an in-flight hull saw it parked back at the shipyard origin -> income coordinator saw the real destination hub unserved -> sent a SECOND hauler -> two haulers on one hub (distinct-hubs failure). (2) FLAKY ARRANGE — old lifetime-1 predicate (PurchaseShip>=1 && navigate>=1) fired on the PURCHASER's own navigate, so the just-bought hull was still DOCKED (not in flight) ~60% of runs -> the bug only bit ~40% -> intermittent (NOT cross-spec contamination as first hypothesized). FIX (assertions STRENGTHENED, not weakened): clock.ts waypointSymbol=destination-while-in-transit; spec arrange pins nav.status==IN_TRANSIT + asserts arrivalBefore truthy; SIGKILL via new daemon.kill() (not graceful SIGTERM which drains + defeats mid-flight kill); clock unit tests updated. Clock unit tests PASS. VERIFYING via full DATA+INCOME+GATE suite (clock.ts is cross-cutting -> full regression check). OPS NOTE: the fix agent left 2 detached verification loops (mt_suite.sh 3-pass + mt_suite_final PASS-A/B/C) leaking harness daemons; reaped (2 clean sweeps). OPEN: confirm 4 twin-OWN acceptance failures (market/register/ship-actions — stateful daemon-integration tests) are pre-existing env, not clock regressions.
close_reason: FIXED + committed aea25d13. Root cause = real twin fidelity bug (twin/src/clock.ts resolveNav served in-transit waypointSymbol as ORIGIN; real-API contract + daemon invariant require DESTINATION -> rebooted daemon mis-adopted in-flight hull as parked-at-origin -> double-dispatch -> two haulers/hub) + flaky arrange (old predicate fired on the purchaser's own navigate, hull still docked ~60%). Fix: destination-while-in-transit + arrange pins nav.status==IN_TRANSIT + real SIGKILL crash (daemon.kill()); assertions STRENGTHENED. income-restart-mid-transit 3/3 green isolation + green in the full 29/29 suite. clock.ts validated by 29/29 bootstrap e2e.

=== RECOMMENDATION ===
MIX, decisively weighted to arbitrage. Concretely:
  • Q1 — DO NOT buy hauler #4 for contracts. Your read survives my refutation: at 3 haulers the contract fleet is at/past optimal. STOP.
  • Q2 — YES, stand up a STANDING arbitrage stream NOW by redeploying the two IDLE haulers (TORWIND-6, -7) to continuous trade tours. Keep TORWIND-5 on contracts as the Phase-1 funding floor. This is the dominant $/hr lever and it ACCELERATES the gate.

=== EVIDENCE ===
CONTRACTS ARE SERIALIZED — hull count is NOT the lever (your read, confirmed):
  - Coordinator runs 1 contract at a time. Contract throughput = 1 / cycle-time. Adding hulls cannot raise it; they only cut buy-leg, which 3 hubs (H54 central, I56 east, E45 central-east) already compress. Cycle-time avg ~8 min (min 0.6, max 42.7, n=39/8h).

A 4th hub cuts buy-leg on ~3% of contracts → marginal throughput ≈ 0. Capex ~305k / 6d needs ~2.1k/hr marginal; closing a 3%-frequency gap can't clear it. #4-for-contracts = NEGATIVE ROI. CONFIRMED.

ARBITRAGE IS THE LEVER — it parallelizes past the serialization ceiling, spreads are huge, and it's ALREADY PROVEN:

  - REALIZED economics (after elasticity, the honest number): FABRICS buy E45 @1003, sold @2434–2560/unit (quote was 5086 — halve quotes for realized). 60u → ~90k NET/trip on a 61u hop (~15–25 min). Sinks RECOVER: K83 back to 5086 quote by 02:02 after our 01:44 dump → lane re-runs. ⇒ ~135k+/hr/hull SUSTAINED, per hull, in parallel.
  - Deep bench of lanes (realized ≈ 40–50% of these quotes): FOOD K83→A1/J58 (~590%), FABRICS E45→K83 (405%), ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY D42→A4 (660%), LAB_INSTRUMENTS C39→A4 (601%), CLOTHING K83→A1, DRUGS J58→H51, MEDICINE F49→A1. Enough distinct lanes for 2–3 hulls WITHOUT colliding.
  - PHASE-1 BONUS: arb cash funds gate materials faster, AND selling into IMPORT sinks grows their depth (feeding thesis) — both accelerate time-to-gate. Arb is not a distraction from the gate; it's the fastest fuel for it.

MARGINAL $/hr, the deciding comparison:
  - Hauler #4 on contracts: ≈ 0 (serialized), −305k capex, never pays back in 6d. NEGATIVE.
  - Idle hull → trade: ≈ 90–135k/hr/hull realized, ZERO capex (own 6/7 already). STRONGLY POSITIVE.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
HIGH that #4-for-contracts is negative ROI and that arbitrage is the bigger lever (both grounded in realized fills + serialization mechanics, not theory). MEDIUM on the sustainable SIZE of the arb stream — bounded by absorption, which I can't fully measure until multiple hulls run it (the manual one-offs didn't stress the sinks).

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND / REFUTATION CAVEATS (attack my own pro-arb call) ===
- ABSORPTION IS THE CEILING, not spreads. Sinks are shallow (tv 20–60/tick) and decay as you dump (5086→2500 realized). Arb $/hr does NOT scale linearly with hulls — 2 hulls on the SAME lane crater it. Size to distinct lanes; cap at measured recovery rate. If sinks decay faster than they recover under a standing stream, per-hull $/hr falls → re-size down.
- CANNIBALIZATION: MEDICINE/DRUGS/EQUIPMENT/FABRICS are BOTH contract goods AND arb lanes. Trade hulls buying them move the markets contracts source from. Prefer non-colliding lanes (FOOD, CLOTHING, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY, LAB_INSTRUMENTS) to avoid self-interference; if arb must use contract goods, expect contract sourcing cost to rise.
- FUNDING FLOOR (Phase 1): contracts are the floor — must not starve. Safe here because contracts need only 1 active hull (serialized); TORWIND-5 + the H54 anchor cover it. But redeploying 6/7 DOES lengthen buy-leg on east-sourced contracts (ANTIMATTER@I56, POLY@E45) when TORWIND-5 must cover them from H54 (105–256u). If contract $/hr degrades measurably, pull one trade hull back toward the east hubs.
- ENGINE CAPABILITY: assumes the trade-fleet-coordinator can run continuous tours on 6/7 while 5 stays on contracts (bead says the engine HAS tour-run/trade-route/arb-run). Confirm the coordinator supports the split + whether a trade hull can be recalled mid-tour for a contract; if hulls are committed per-tour, keep tours SHORT so contract coverage recovers quickly.
- If a durable/armored hull is later acquired, the J58 lanes (DRUGS/FOOD/MEDICINE sinks, 600%+) open up for it — currently pirate-risky for light hulls to sit on.
- REAL STOP CONDITION: contract fleet = 3 (done). Trade fleet = grow while measured per-hull trade $/hr (net of decay + fuel) exceeds ~0 marginal contract-hull value; stop when a new trade hull's lane can't clear its own sink faster than it decays.
CORRECTION (Admiral 2026-07-13) — my over-provisioned/idle-capital hypothesis was WRONG, disregard it. CORRECT MODEL: one active contract at a time is BY DESIGN (1 busy hauler expected). Idle haulers are balanced across hubs as pre-positioned SOURCE COVERAGE; when a contract is accepted the NEAREST idle hull sources it, compressing the buy-leg -> shorter cycle-time -> higher $/hr. So MORE haulers/hubs INCREASE $/hr by covering more source origins, UP TO saturation of the contract-source distribution. REFRAMED ASKS: (1) given the ACTUAL contract-source waypoint distribution in X1-VB74, how many hubs/haulers are needed to cover it — marginal cycle-time gain per added hub and the saturation point? (2) marginal $/hr of hauler/hub #4 vs ~305k capex + ~6d payback (positive until source-coverage saturates?); (3) ARBITRAGE TRADEOFF: using idle-gap contract haulers for arb PULLS them off-position (degrades contract cycle-time) — is arb revenue per idle-gap worth that positioning cost, or is a DEDICATED trade hull (new capex) the right way to run a parallel arb stream? Recommend contract-scale / hold / dedicated-arb-hull / mix, with sizing.

title: gc builtin 'claude' provider model choices are stale (opus→4-7, sonnet→4-6, haiku→4-5)
description: The installed gc binary's builtin claude provider exposes a closed model enum whose named choices map to stale model generations, and option_defaults hard-rejects any value not in that enum (ValidateOptionDefaults in gascity-src/internal/config/options.go). If gc is upgraded with a refreshed builtin schema, revisit whether these custom providers can collapse back into plain option_defaults. Upstream fix lives in gascity-src (separate repo), not this one.

title: DaemonServer.getAPIClient() news up a fresh client, bypassing the shared rate limiter and metri
description:
Level L6 · effort S · analyzer value 5 · judge-adjusted 3.5 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_construction.go, internal/adapters/grpc/daemon_server.go, cmd/spacetraders-daemon/main.go

PROBLEM: getAPIClient() (container_ops_construction.go:162-164) returns api.NewSpaceTradersClient() — a brand-new client with its OWN token-bucket rate limiter and NO metrics collector — called 3x within a single construction flow. These ad-hoc clients do not share the global SpaceTraders request budget, so construction traffic (the jump-gate delivery path) can push the account past the 429 threshold the rest of the daemon carefully throttles against, and their calls are invisible to Prometheus.

PROPOSAL: Inject a single api.APIClient (with rate limiter + metrics) into DaemonServer once (it already receives collaborators in NewDaemonServer, daemon_server.go:90) and have getAPIClient() return that shared instance. First step: add an apiClient field to DaemonServer, wire it from main.go's existing api.NewSpaceTradersClient() (main.go:142), and return s.apiClient.

TEST PREREQ: Assert construction ops route through the injected client (a test double), verifying a single shared rate limiter — cheap once the field exists.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Rate-limiter half of the problem is real and verified: getAPIClient() (container_ops_construction.go:162-164) news up api.NewSpaceTradersClient(), each of which builds its own rate.NewLimiter(2,30) (client.go:85), while main.go threads a single shared apiClient (main.go:142) as the daemon's throttled budget. Construction thus opens independent token buckets outside that budget — genuine bypass on the priority gate path, and the fix (inject shared client, return s.apiClient) eliminates rather than relocates it. However the metrics half is FALSE: getMetricsCollector() (client.go:106-118) falls back to metrics.GetGlobalAPICollector(), set at daemon_server.go:223, so these clients already report to Prometheus. behavior_safe: rate.Limiter is goroutine-safe; adding one field + one NewDaemonServer param (single call site main.go:398) and returning it is a reversible injection whose only runtime change is the intended throttling — the test-double prereq is cheap. Value trimmed to 3: the concern is real and mission-relevant but overstated (false metrics claim) and bounded in impact — construction clients are minted per-RPC, not high-frequency pollers, so excess request pressure is intermitt
