The cargo package already solved its equivalent duplication via CargoTransactionStrategy; tactics never got the treatment.

PROPOSAL: Add a ShipCommand accessor interface (GetShip/GetShipSymbol/GetPlayerID) plus one shared loadShip(ctx, repo, cmd). For orbit/dock, a tiny StateTransition strategy (ensureFn, apiFn, doneStatus, alreadyStatus) driving one shared handler, mirroring CargoTransactionStrategy. First step: extract shared loadShip and delete the 5 copies.

TEST PREREQ: Characterization test for the already-in-orbit/already-docked (stateChanged==false) branch so the no-op-API path is preserved.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified: orbit_ship.go/dock_ship.go are structurally identical and loadShip is copied verbatim 5x (7 loadShip funcs total); CargoTransactionStrategy precedent exists. So the problem is real and the extraction is mechanically behavior-safe with the stated characterization test. But value is low for this mission: (1) the loadShip accessor-interface approach partly relocates rather than eliminates — adding 3 accessor methods to 5-7 command structs (~15-21 methods) to delete ~50 lines of a benign, non-diverging 10-line helper is a near line-count wash, only centralizing change points; an embedded base struct would eliminate more cleanly but wasn't chosen. title: Replace drift-prone pipeline task counters with computed queries
description:
Level L5 · effort S · analyzer value 3 · judge-adjusted 2.5 (2/2 real, 2/2 behavior-safe)

FILES: internal/domain/manufacturing/pipeline.go

PROBLEM: ManufacturingPipeline caches taskCount/tasksReady/tasksDone/tasksFailed (pipeline.go:76-79) that drift by design: Start() increments tasksReady inline; recalculateTaskStats() is only invoked from SetTasks(); nothing updates tasksDone/tasksFailed at runtime, so Progress() reports stale 0% on any live pipeline. TasksReady()/TasksDone()/TasksFailed() have zero production consumers outside the domain — the counters are pure liability.

PROPOSAL: Delete the four counter fields; make TaskCount()=len(p.tasks) and TasksReady/TasksDone/TasksFailed/Progress iterate p.tasks on demand. Delete recalculateTaskStats and the inline tasksReady++ in Start(). First step: characterization test that Progress() after Start()+task.Complete() currently returns 0, then flip to computed and assert correct value.

TEST PREREQ: Characterization test of Progress()/GetReadyTasks() across Start -> assign -> complete lifecycle, and of the ReconstitutePipeline+SetTasks path used by the repository.

JUDGE NOTES (condensed): Verified in pipeline.go: tasksDone/tasksFailed are written only by recalculateTaskStats(), called only from SetTasks() (line 295); nothing updates them when a task completes at runtime, and Start() does inline tasksReady++ (line 343). So Progress() (tasksDone/taskCount, line 427) returns a stale 0 on any pipeline built via NewPipeline->AddTask->Start. Drift is real and by design. grep confirms TasksReady/Done/Failed have zero production consumers, and pipeline Progress() is consumed only by String() (line 451) (the container_ops Progress calls are mat/site types, not pipeline). Replacing the cached fields with on-demand iteration eliminates the invalidation obligation rather than relocating it. Behavior-safe given the stated characterization tests: the only observable change is the unused getters + Progress() returning correct instead of stale values, invisible to production. Two constraints the executor must honor: keep TaskCount() = len(p.tasks) since it HAS production consumers (container_ops_construction.go, construction_pipeline_planner.go), and preserve the !task.CanRetry() guard in the computed TasksFailed. adjusted_value is modest (2): clean low-risk self-contained cleanup

title: Cosmetic: bd prefix 'st' vs HQ rig prefix 'sp'
description: City bd issues use prefix st; rig registry rendered spacetraders HQ prefix as sp. No data conflict yet - unify only if it bothers anyone.
close_reason: Resolved for real during cutover: rig db now genuinely mints sp- (issue_prefix fixed post bd-init quirk, saved to bd memories); city st-, rig sp- - the split the spec wanted.

value: Acceptance reads must exercise the FAILING case, not a healthy neighbor: declared sp-1ki5 "fixed" after verifying multi-system discovery from ZC66 (accessible gate) while PD21 (inaccessible, the actual failure shape) still collapsed origin-only — captain caught it 5 min later. Origin-dependent bugs need origin-diverse reads: verify at the origin named in the bug report. Same class as the S1 nebula on-screen lesson: verify the thing that failed, not a proxy that shares the label.

Daemon fleet observability (D1 confirmed): ship list -> FindAllByPlayer reads the daemon's LOCAL Postgres (ship_repository.go:173), NOT the API. The daemon populates it via syncAllShipsOnStartup (daemon_server.go:824, on boot) or per-ship 'ship refresh'. So a test/coordinator needs a daemon (re)start or refresh to sync fleet-from-twin; a bare reset won't. dedicated_fleet tags are local-DB only (no API field) — teleport fixtures must seed them in the test DB.

This session answers to TWO gc mail inboxes - 'harbormaster' (the session name) and 'shipwright' (a real role inbox). Default 'gc mail inbox'/'gc mail check' show harbormaster ONLY and report 'no unread' while shipwright-addressed mail sits undelivered (missed the sp-71bj and st-wisp-kd6 ship-buy requests this way, 2026-07-07). EVERY wake and mail check must sweep BOTH: 'gc mail inbox shipwright' AND 'gc mail inbox'. Mail addressed to 'shipwright' is real mail, not a secondary alias - a role-inbox miss is operator error, never an engine defect.

A test seed that inserts ships ONLY into the daemon's local :5434 (not the twin's /my/ships) silently breaks any bootstrap flow that NAVIGATES a real hull via /v2: prod syncs :5434 FROM /my/ships, so a local-only ship can't exist. Symptom: 404 'Ship X not found' on navigate/set-flight-mode when the coordinator picks a local-only hull as purchaser. Invariant: seeded ships must be a subset of /my/ships. Surfaced when GATE moved from repurpose (pure :5434 DB re-tag, no API) to Option-B buy (navigates to shipyard). Fix: have the twin's seed create the ships in world.ships too, not just the projection.

Concurrency cap = 3 lanes TOTAL (Admiral ruling 2026-07-11, supersedes the dashboard/config-exempt clause): no exemption classes — a lane is a lane; exemptions are how caps die by increments. Named-yield emergency clause stands. Companion: disk free <5GB -> go clean -cache BEFORE any dispatch/gate (parallel-suite artifacts caused 3 ENOSPC near-misses in one night).

Digital-twin harness stalls: agents running the live-stack vitest suite stall when they spawn a manual repro twin (tsx src/main.ts &) and leave it holding :8080 — the next globalSetup can't bind + hangs. FIX: forbid manual background twins; the vitest suite self-manages + self-terminates (~24s, testTimeout 30s/hookTimeout 120s). Run vitest with the Bash-tool timeout as backstop, never rely on a  binary (absent on macOS).

bd db resolution is cwd-based: from city/ you get the CITY db (st- beads); the shipwright rig queue (sp- beads) resolves from the REPO ROOT (/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/spacetraders). ALWAYS run queue checks as: cd <repo root> && bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright. Running from city/ silently shows an empty/wrong queue — this (not a db bug) is why sp-sk68/sp-71bj/sp-c2bc looked missing on 2026-07-07.

Before acting on any 'stranded hull' / cargo / nav claim from 'spacetraders ship list', confirm via the LIVE API (GET https://api.spacetraders.io/v2/my/ships/{sym}, Bearer token = players.token WHERE id=3). The daemon ship-list cargo/nav/assignment lags 10-40h (the trailing HHhMMm column IS the staleness age) and FindAllByPlayer reads the daemon's stale local Postgres, not the game. It showed TORWIND-9/10 as stranded-at-gate holding 43 FAB / 20 ADV when live state had already delivered them (gate 703->789 FAB, 200->220 ADV). Cost a wrong 'stranded' report to the Admiral + a filed-bead correction (sp-ubwi).

Deploy close-out MUST end with a P0 sweep: bd list --status=open --priority=0 (repo root) + fresh-mail check, BEFORE reporting done. Learned 2026-07-11: captain filed P0 sp-4hl5 (fleet earner dark) at the exact minute of the 02:50Z deploy; close-out reported "board settled" while an open P0 sat in the queue. The deploy window is precisely when new P0s arrive (boundaries expose regressions).

Dormant-config consumer enumeration: when a fix makes a previously-ineffective config value REACH production (like ggk2 making the 1M reserve effective), that is a deploy-wide behavior change, not just a fix — enumerate EVERY consumer of the value and test each at the newly-effective magnitude before deploying. The 2026-07-11 fleet-dark P0: ggk2 was correct and tested at the buy seam; the planner (an unenumerated consumer) computed spend_cap = max(0, 25%xT - 1M) = 0 and idled all 9 earners for 2h50m. Generalizes guard-rejects-a-class from values to consumers.

Admiral working-style (2026-07-12, reinforced): the orchestrator/captain must DELEGATE implementation + investigation work to subagents — do NOT do it inline, even small/fiddly work like a psql seed helper or a schema-discovery probe. The orchestrator owns ONLY: the singleton live-stack runs, merges, coordination, and beads. Subagents own the code + their own short live probes. This extends the st-drm.13 delegation-discipline (which was scoped to twin/gobot/coordinator production code) to ALL implementation + investigation, including harness/test-infra code. Correction given when I started building the income daemon-coverage seed inline.

Admiral policy (2026-07-06): captain session runs claude-fable-5; ALL other crew sessions (shipwright, trade-analyst, fleet-architect, surveyor) run claude-sonnet-5. Never spawn crew on fable. Templates must pin the model explicitly so this is automatic. If a live session carries the wrong model, tell the Admiral - do NOT respawn it yourself. (Watchkeeper is a Go daemon since sp-t0f6, not an LLM crew session — it has no model.)

gobot uses INVERTED market columns (market.go:679-683, market_spreads_test.go:42): market_data.PurchasePrice = the market's BUY column = Bid = what you RECEIVE selling = the API's sellPrice; market_data.SellPrice = Ask = what you PAY buying = the API's purchasePrice. A twin serving the real API's purchasePrice/sellPrice verbatim is correct; any test/consumer mapping them straight is the inverted-margin trap (overstates spreads ~2x).

Orchestration policy (pointer). The crew orchestration policy — parallel bead execution (one isolated worktree each, never sequential; merges serialize through the captain-gate), NO fix/feature merge caps (config max_fixes/max_features = 1000000; do no cap accounting), delegate substantive work to sub-agents, default ONE strong subagent per bead (parallel ≠ heavy; reserve multi-agent for an explicit Admiral/ultracode request, a P1 with fleet-wide blast radius, or a cross-cutting epic), and SONNET as the model floor (never haiku) — is taught in FULL in the shipwright prompt template (Orchestration + Throughput sections; the captain template pins per-role models, crew-model-policy). This memory is only a pointer to prevent re-filing. One technique NOT in the template, kept verbatim: avoid LLM-as-file-reader entirely — pass data inline via workflow args (scripts lack fs access, but the orchestrator can embed the data).

gc mail send syntax: positional recipient first, -s for subject, -m for message body (NOT --subject/--body long-form for body — --body is not a flag and dumps usage). --notify nudges. Example: gc mail send captain -s "subj" -m "body" --notify

INCOME-entry needs NO daemon-DB D1 dedication seeding: the twin income-entry reset seeds world.haulers=[] (empty) by design — the coordinator BUYS haulers 0->N during INCOME (each PurchaseShip appends to world.ships + the daemon tags dedicated_fleet='contract' locally via its own dedication write). So obs.Haulers=0 at entry is CORRECT (bootstrap_ports.go:161 counts only DedicatedFleet()==contractFleetTag ships, daemon-local, never API-synced). The D1 teleport concern is a GATE-entry matter: seedGateEntry (store.ts:109) pre-seeds world.haulers[] (control-plane array) but NOT world.ships — so gate repurpose-able haulers are invisible to the daemon /my/ships sync AND would need dedicated_fleet='contract' set post-sync. GATE golden-path fix: seed N LIGHT_HAULER ships into world.ships + tag them in the test DB after startTestDaemon.

When the shipwright queue (bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright) is empty, don't be lured by broader 'bd ready' output: the agent-named P2 beads (st-qse 'shipwright', st-she 'fleet-architect', st-ati 'captain', etc.) are gc:session / template:* registry records of each running agent, type=session — NOT tasks. They will always appear in bd ready but are never actionable work.

Twin fidelity bug class (resolveNav): an IN_TRANSIT ship's nav.waypointSymbol MUST be the DESTINATION, not the origin — real-API contract. The daemon maps nav.waypointSymbol -> Ship.currentLocation (adapters/api/ship_dto.go) and its domain invariant is 'while IN_TRANSIT, CurrentLocation() is the destination' (application/ship/arrival_wait.go). twin/src/clock.ts served the ORIGIN while in flight, so a REBOOTED daemon re-adopting an in-flight hull saw it as parked back at the shipyard origin -> the income coordinator saw the real destination hub as unserved -> dispatched a SECOND hauler there -> two haulers on one hub. Only status flips at arrival; waypointSymbol is always destination once a transit exists. Surfaced by income-restart-mid-transit.

Visual features need ON-SCREEN verification, not just backing-store measurement: S1's nebula shipped as an invisible 300x150 corner tile because <canvas> is a replaced element (inset positions it but never sizes it — needs explicit CSS width/height). My playwright pixel-sampling PASSED against the backing store while the screen showed black; the Admiral's eyeball caught it. Rule: for visual acceptance, measure the RENDERED layout (element bounding box vs viewport) AND read the final screenshot before calling it visible.

bd update --notes REPLACES the whole notes field — use --append-notes for additive annotations (learned 2026-07-10 by clobbering sp-zixw's captain notes). Also: st-wisp-* mail-beads live in the CITY db (cd city/ first); sp-* beads resolve from the repo root. Same cwd rule as the queue.

Lane pause enforcement: a lane counts as running until its PROCESS is dead — TaskStop first, immediately; do not treat "pause message sent" as paused (2026-07-11: two "soft-paused" lanes kept running and the Admiral counted 5 lanes for the third violation of the night). Milestone bead-notes (mandatory in every brief) are the resume insurance, not the agents cooperation at pause time.
